title: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Warrensville Heights, OH
metaTitle: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Warrensville Heights, OH
metaDescription: Section 8 apartments for rent in Warrensville Heights, OH: the local voucher-protection ordinance, how CMHA vouchers work, and nearby rentals.
primaryKeyword: section 8 apartments for rent Warrensville Heights ohio
secondaryKeywords: voucher friendly rentals near shaker square
answerBox: Warrensville Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb southeast of Shaker Heights and Beachwood, and reporting names it as one of a small number of local suburbs with an ordinance protecting Housing Choice Voucher holders from source-of-income discrimination. CMHA administers Section 8 countywide. We don't yet manage units inside Warrensville Heights, but manage voucher-friendly homes nearby in Buckeye-Shaker.

sections.h2: Can I rent a Section 8 apartment in Warrensville Heights?
body: In terms of legal protection, yes: reporting identifies Warrensville Heights as one of a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs with an ordinance generally barring landlords from refusing an applicant solely for using a Housing Choice Voucher. Rent Finder Cleveland does not currently manage apartment units inside Warrensville Heights itself, though. Our closest managed homes sit just to the northwest in **Buckeye-Shaker** (ZIP 44120), along the corridor where Cleveland's Shaker Square area meets Shaker Heights.
[Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability nearby, or tell our leasing team if a Warrensville Heights address is specifically what you need.

sections.h2: Warrensville Heights' voucher protection, and how it compares
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring a landlord to accept a Housing Choice Voucher, and neither does the City of Cleveland — Cleveland's 2024 "Pay to Stay" ordinance dropped source-of-income provisions during negotiation before it passed. Warrensville Heights is different: reporting describes it, along with Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, and University Heights, as one of a small group of Cuyahoga County suburbs that has adopted its own local protection. That's a meaningful distinction from neighboring suburbs like Garfield Heights and Maple Heights, which are not reported to have equivalent ordinances.

Confirm current protections directly with the city before relying on this, since municipal ordinances can change. Our [source-of-income guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/source-of-income-discrimination-cleveland-ohio/) breaks down the jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction picture across Greater Cleveland.

sections.h2: How the CMHA voucher process works
body: Whether or not you end up renting inside Warrensville Heights, the voucher itself comes from [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority, which administers Section 8 for the entire county. CMHA's preliminary application is open online year-round with no closing date, and applicants are chosen from that list through periodic random mini-lotteries rather than strict order — a name might come up within days, or it may take months, according to CMHA's own guidance. Once a household holds a voucher, the specific unit still has to pass a housing-quality inspection; HUD is transitioning that standard from HQS to NSPIRE, with a final deadline of February 1, 2027 for the [Housing Choice Voucher program](https://www.hud.gov/helping-americans/housing-choice-vouchers).

Warrensville Heights has no rail station of its own, but it sits just south of the Green Line's eastern terminus near Green Road, on the Shaker Heights/Beachwood border, giving nearby residents rail access into Shaker Square and Downtown's Tower City hub. [RTA's regular bus network](https://www.riderta.com/routes) also runs through Warrensville Heights directly along Warrensville Center Road, connecting to both the Blue and Green light-rail lines.

Rent Finder Cleveland's 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, and Buckeye-Shaker is the closest of those neighborhoods to Warrensville Heights, sitting along the same Blue and Green rail corridor. Every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and stays HUD-inspection-ready.

[Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability near Warrensville Heights, or visit our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full local application walkthrough.

Every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Tell our team what you need and we'll point you to the nearest fit.

faq.q: Does Warrensville Heights protect Section 8 voucher holders?
faq.a: Reporting identifies Warrensville Heights as one of a small number of Cuyahoga County suburbs with a local ordinance generally barring landlords from refusing an applicant solely for using a Housing Choice Voucher, alongside Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, and University Heights. Confirm current rules with the city before relying on this.

faq.q: Who administers Section 8 vouchers for Warrensville Heights?
faq.a: CMHA, the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority, administers Housing Choice Vouchers countywide, including Warrensville Heights. Its preliminary application is open online year-round, with applicants selected through periodic random mini-lotteries rather than strict first-come-first-served order.

faq.q: How is Warrensville Heights different from neighboring Garfield Heights or Maple Heights for Section 8?
faq.a: Warrensville Heights is reported to have its own source-of-income protection ordinance, while Garfield Heights and Maple Heights are not. That means a landlord in those neighboring suburbs can generally decline an applicant for using a voucher, while one in Warrensville Heights generally cannot do so solely for that reason.

faq.q: What's the closest Section 8-friendly rental to Warrensville Heights?
faq.a: Buckeye-Shaker (ZIP 44120) is our closest concentration of voucher-friendly homes to Warrensville Heights, along the same RTA Blue and Green light-rail corridor that runs through the area.
